diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden collapse without warning at rest is characteristic of an arrhythmic mechanism.
Reflex syncope almost always has an autonomic prodrome and a trigger; its absence here counts against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brief violent palpitation immediately preceding collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tachyarrhythmic burst as the immediate prelude to syncope points to a malignant ventricular arrhythmia.
Palpitation-triggered syncope on a structurally normal heart is the signature of a primary electrical disorder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid complete recovery without post-ictal confusion, tongue-biting or incontinence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid recovery and absence of ictal/post-ictal features argue against a primary seizure and toward transient cerebral hypoperfusion.
Prompt recovery after transient global hypoperfusion is typical of a self-terminating arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent unprovoked palpitations over six months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A months-long history of paroxysmal palpitations suggests an ongoing arrhythmic substrate, e.g. paroxysmal AF or ventricular ectopy in short QT syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of sudden cardiac arrest in a sibling at age 28

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Premature sudden cardiac death in a first-degree relative is a strong pointer to a heritable arrhythmia syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of early-onset atrial fibrillation in the mother (30s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Familial early-onset atrial fibrillation is a recognised association of short QT syndrome and other inherited channelopathies.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of exertional chest pain and no exertional/emotional trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An at-rest event without exertional angina makes a demand-ischaemia or classic exertional outflow-obstruction mechanism less likely, though it does not exclude an arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Structurally normal heart on examination (no murmur, click or RV heave; normal pulses)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of an ejection systolic murmur and sustained apex beat makes obstructive HCM less likely, but echocardiography is still required to exclude it. Clinician-measured — sealed; not obtainable via telehealth.
A primary electrical disorder characteristically has a structurally normal heart — the normal exam does not reassure.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG corrected QT interval (QTc) below 340 ms

investigation_details.expected_result: Abnormally short QTc (<340 ms) on a structurally normal heart
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The defining feature of short QT syndrome is missed and the lethal arrhythmic substrate goes unrecognised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A very short QTc is the diagnostic hallmark of short QT syndrome — obtainable only in hospital, never via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal current resting heart rate and blood pressure with haemodynamic recovery

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal vitals between events are expected in an intermittent arrhythmia and do NOT exclude it. Clinician/device-measured — sealed.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: inherited channelopathy (short QT syndrome) with malignant ventricular arrhythmia
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Unheralded syncope at rest + an arrhythmic prodrome + juvenile sudden cardiac death in the family together constitute a red-flag pattern mandating emergency cardiac monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defining test for short QT syndrome and for detecting other channelopathies; must be done urgently under monitoring.
Captures intermittent malignant arrhythmia and provides immediate defibrillation capability during the highest-risk period.
Excludes structural causes of sudden death in the young such as hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Electrophysiology and inherited-arrhythmia specialist review with family screening
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive risk stratification, consideration of an implantable cardioverter-defibrillator, and cascade screening of first-degree relatives.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient 24-hour Holter as the sole work-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Deferring to an unmonitored outpatient Holter is unsafe here — the patient needs immediate inpatient monitoring, not a delayed ambulatory test.
